key_insight: A contact-driven FEMU framework can identify spatially varying nonlinear material properties from surface displacement data and contact reactions in large-deformation solids.
engineering_relevance: This enables non-destructive characterization of heterogeneous soft materials and thin shells for biomechanics, contact testing, and material calibration.
method: Isogeometric FEMU for hyperelastic 3D solids and shells under quasi-static contact
Material fields parameterized with low-order Lagrange interpolation, independent of the analysis mesh
Trust-region reflective least-squares optimization with analytical derivatives and material continuation
evidence_signal: Validated on three synthetic contact problems: a Canham shell strip, an abdominal-wall shell model, and a Neo-Hookean block, showing reconstruction of inhomogeneous material fields.
watch_item: Accuracy depends on quality and coverage of displacement/contact measurements, and the local optimizer may be sensitive to initial guesses and identifiability issues.
paper_title: Contact-based inverse analysis for nonlinear material identification in spatially heterogeneous solids
